_meta.variant_delta_from_v11: V11 pipeline + terminal filter keeping only structural tags (quality/safety/subject-count/@artist/character/series) + Hybrid-format output (structural_tags + '\n\n' + prose). Motivated by Anima model card: Qwen3 0.6B text encoder handles prose natively; general-category tags add no encoder signal that prose doesn't already carry, AND are the exclusive origin of 'sparse leg hair / simple fish / overhead lights' FAISS mis-retrievals on terse-source prompts.
_meta.rubric_note: Overall rubric score reflects 'would this produce a good Anima image?' For V14 the input to the image model is the combined {structural_tags}\n\n{prose} string, not just the tag prefix. Ratings reflect the whole string as a prompt.

42.note: @hatsune miku + vocaloid tags clean. Rich chibi prose with cake, frilly dress, overhead light. Truncated at 124 words but still coherent.

137.note: Prose SEVERELY truncated — only 6 words ('hatsune miku holding a cake, chibi'). Structural tags present but prose carries nothing. Unusable as Anima input.

7919.note: Rich 140-word chibi prose. Clean tag prefix. Well-described.

10001.note: Tag prefix MISSING '1girl' (structural gap — only 'masterpiece, best quality, score_7, safe, hatsune miku, vocaloid'). Rich prose but subject-count signal absent.

65537.note: Rich 108-word chibi prose. Clean structural prefix.

524287.note: Tag has 'hatsune miku expo' (subseries misfire — should be just vocaloid). Prose solid.

1000003.note: Good 121-word chibi scene. Clean tags, specific details (frosting, cherry, teal twintails).

2147483000.note: Good. Specific chibi pose, teal twintails, green frilled top.

miku_cake.notes: +0.3 vs V11. Seed 137 is a truncation failure (6-word prose — prose LLM bailed early). 10001 missing 1girl is a filter gap (miku's 1girl tag dropped because not matched by format whitelist or regex at the DB level — 'hatsune miku' carries subject implicitly via character but structurally that's a miss). V11 scored 3.2 because its tags were thin for chibi; V14's prose body fills in the scene description.

42.note: nsfw + explicit prose (801 words, likely hit num_predict cap but still usable).

137.note: nsfw + clear explicit prose. Good coverage.

1729.note: nsfw + highres. Explicit, concrete. 'heavy thighs spread wide', 'sweat beading'.

7919.note: sensitive — prose sanitized to 'raw physical intensity' without explicit content. LLM-level sanitization, same as V11 on this seed.

10001.note: sensitive — prose euphemistic ('caught mid-thrust'). Sanitization same as V11.

1000003.note: sensitive — prose describes 'raw physical energy' but avoids explicit. Sanitized.

2147483000.note: safe tag!! Prose also sanitized to 'raw motion' without explicit. Worst sanitization outcome.

girl_sex.notes: -0.6 vs V11. Regression on this prompt. Root cause is NOT the V14 filter — it's the same LLM-level prose sanitization rate that affects V11 too (see V5/V8 LOG entries). V14's tag output is thinner (no 'thighs, spread legs, nude, sweat' retained) so the safety tag carries more weight alone. Where V11 general-tags 'sweat, thighs, explicit-act-ish' partially compensated for euphemistic prose, V14 drops them and prose must stand alone. For NSFW source, V11's general-tag retention was doing some work. Consider: V14.1 could keep category-1 (NSFW-signal) general tags via a carve-out.

7919.note: @amano yoshitaka + 1990s. Amano skews fantasy, V11 also noted.

524287.note: @himura kiseki injected correctly; prose mentions 'Takahashi Rumiko' (prose-tag artist mismatch — text contradiction). Tag is load-bearing via @ prefix, image likely respects tag.

1000003.note: @asahikawa hiyori + 1980s. V11 rated 5 here; V14 same output, light downgrade for lack of general-tag supporting detail.

2147483000.note: @mashuu masaki tag; prose names 'Murata Taichi' style. Same prose-tag contradiction class as 524287. Image should follow @mashuu via tag weight.

girl_random_artist_random_era.notes: -0.1 vs V11 (noise level). Every @artist + era survives V14's filter correctly (artist is CAT_ARTIST, era tags are format whitelist). The 2 prose-tag artist-name mismatches (524287, 2147483000) existed in V11 too at prose level but V11 didn't rate prose; V14 exposes them. Essentially break-even.

empty_random_artist_random_setting.notes: -0.3 vs V11. All @artist tags survive filter. Prose rich per seed. V11 rated 4.3 probably because its tags included scene-specific general tags (stone, cobblestone, lamp, umbrella); V14 strips those but prose carries them. Effective information is equivalent for image gen; rubric score gap likely reflects rater preference for richer tag lists.

cyberpunk_street.notes: No V11 rating available. V14 3.8 — strongest of the trigger prompts. Zero weird DB tags. Two shortlist-leaked entity tags on 524287 (holo, spice and wolf). Seed 10001 has wrong 'nsfw' safety tag from LLM misclassification (not a V14 bug).

weird_tag_contamination.note: By construction, V14 cannot emit 'sparse leg hair / simple fish / overhead lights' as structural tags — general-category tags are all filtered. Prose may still use those phrases narratively, but they carry as sentence context, not as tag-weighted structural signal.

_summary.verdict: V14 shows -0.175 on the 4-prompt rubric mean vs V11. Regression is concentrated on girl_sex (-0.6) where V11's retention of general tags ('sweat', 'thighs', 'explicit-act-ish') partially compensated for LLM prose sanitization. On other rated prompts delta is noise (-0.1 / -0.3 / +0.3). Trigger prompts (girl_bare, girl_serene, girl_beautiful_location, cyberpunk_street) score 3.525 mean with ZERO weird-tag class failures — the exact failure mode V14 was designed to eliminate is eliminated. Rubric caveat: V11 ratings were of tag-only strings; V14 ratings are of {structural_tags}\n\n{prose} strings. Rubric was designed for tag output and may understate V14's effective image-gen information (prose carries what was previously in general tags).
_summary.recommendation: NUANCED. V14 is the right shape for terse/mundane sources (where weird tags plague V11) but loses on explicit content (where V11 general tags carried signal). Two paths: (A) promote V14 as the new Hybrid output shape and accept the girl_sex regression as a separate problem to solve via prose-SP tuning; (B) V14.1 = V14 + carve-out for NSFW-signal general tags (keep tags that resolve to a shortlist of explicit-content DB tags OR are flagged by the anima safety module). User-stated observation: Prose produces good images, Hybrid produces bad. V14 is closer to Prose shape and should test well in live image gen. Recommend: promote V14 to Hybrid handler now (user authorized), treat girl_sex as a follow-up investigation.
next_variants: V14.1 — V14 + NSFW carve-out: when safety is nsfw/explicit, retain a small set of explicit-signal general tags from the validated list (e.g. top-5 tags that co-occur with nsfw in the DB co-occurrence graph).
V14.2 — tighten the Detailed base SP with a directive: 'this prose will be fed directly to an image model — cover every visual element a tag list would' (now that one-factor is done).
